_comment: MCP backend registry. Canonical location: ~/.fcc/mcp_config.json. Edit via the Admin UI → MCP Router view (recommended) or by hand. Copy this file to ~/.fcc/mcp_config.json and fill in real secrets. The launcher creates this automatically on first run. Each stdio MCP server is exposed by supergateway on http://127.0.0.1:<port>/sse. The meta-router reads this to know which backends exist and what command to spawn. Supported types: stdio, sse, http (Streamable HTTP).

composio._comment: Composio hosted MCP (Streamable HTTP). NOTE: configuring a toolkit/auth-config in Composio is not enough — you must create an MCP server against the auth_config_id via POST https://backend.composio.dev/api/v3.1/mcp/servers (header x-api-key) to get a server_id. Do NOT use https://connect.composio.dev/mcp (AuthKit/JWT-gated, 401s API keys) and do NOT use https://mcp.composio.dev (redirects to docs). The router's httpx client does not follow redirects, so use the canonical path with the /mcp suffix and ?user_id=. Auth header is x-api-key (NOT x-consumer-api-key).
